resource.nopermission: You don't have the permissions to view this resource.
blueprints.home.library.title: Your Library
blueprints.home.library.subtitle: Games that are available to create new servers immediately.
profile.appearence.title: Appearence
profile.appearence.description: Choose between light, dark or system theme to match your preferences or device settings.
profile.language.title: Language
profile.language.description: Select your preferred language for the interface and system messages.
units.create.general.title: Choose a Name
units.create.general.description: Give your server an identity. You can always rename it later.
units.create.name.label: Server name
units.create.blueprint.title: Pick a Game
units.create.blueprint.description: What will this server run? Choose from ready-to-play blueprints.
units.create.configuration.title: Configure
units.create.configuration.description: Network and game-specific settings for your server.
units.create.configuration.placeholder: Pick a game first to unlock configuration options.
units.create.resources.title: Resources
units.create.resources.description: Runtime and hardware limits. Defaults work for most games.
units.create.resources.runtime.label: Runtime
units.create.resources.runtime.dockerDes: Runs inside an isolated Docker container with its own filesystem and network. Recommended for most games.
units.create.resources.hardware.label: Hardware limits
units.create.resources.hardware.memory.d: Determines how much data the server can hold in memory at once. Higher values reduce lag with more players or larger worlds. Most games run well with 1–4 GB.
units.create.resources.hardware.cpu.name: CPU
units.create.resources.hardware.cpu.deta: Controls how much processor time the server can use. 100% equals one full CPU core. Increase for modded servers or heavy world generation.
units.create.resources.hardware.disk.det: Space for server files, worlds, backups, and plugins. Larger worlds and frequent backups need more disk. Cannot be reduced once allocated.
units.create.resources.hardware.swap.det: When RAM runs out, the system uses swap as slower overflow memory. Prevents crashes during memory spikes but with reduced performance. Set to 0 to disable.
